segments.text: After these things Jesus went over the sea of Galilee, which is the sea of Tiberias.
And a great multitude followed him, because they saw his miracles which he did on them that were diseased.
And Jesus went up into a mountain, and there he sat with his disciples.
And the passover, a feast of the Jews, was nigh.
When Jesus then lifted up his eyes, and saw a great company come unto him,
he saith unto Philip
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And this he said to prove him: for he himself knew what he would do.
Two hundred pennyworth of bread is not sufficient for them, that every one of them may take a little.
segments.speaker: Philip

segments.text: One of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peter's brother, saith unto him,
There is a lad here, which hath five barley loaves, and two small fishes: but what are they among so many?
segments.speaker: Andrew

segments.text: And Jesus said, Now there was much grass in the place.
So the men sat down, in number about five thousand.
And Jesus took the loaves; and when he had given thanks, he distributed to the disciples, and the disciples to them that were set down; and likewise of the fishes as much as they would.
When they were filled,
he said unto his disciples
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then those men, when they had seen the miracle that Jesus did,
said, This is of a truth that prophet that should come into the world.
segments.speaker: The People

segments.text: When Jesus therefore perceived that they would come and take him by force, to make him a king, he departed again into a mountain himself alone.
Rabbi, when camest thou hither?
segments.speaker: The People

segments.text: Verily, verily, I say unto you, Ye seek me, not because ye saw the miracles, but because ye did eat of the loaves, and were filled.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: What shall we do, that we might work the works of God?
segments.speaker: The People

segments.text: This is the work of God, that ye believe on him whom he hath sent.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Lord, evermore give us this bread.
segments.speaker: The People

segments.text: I am the bread of life: he that cometh to me shall never hunger; and he that believeth on me shall never thirst.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The Jews then murmured at him, because he said, I am the bread which came down from heaven.
These things said he in the synagogue, as he taught in Capernaum.
From that time many of his disciples went back, and walked no more with him.
Lord, to whom shall we go? thou hast the words of eternal life.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: And we believe and are sure that thou art that Christ, the Son of the living God.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: He spake of Judas Iscariot the son of Simon: for he it was that should betray him, being one of the twelve.
